title: Guru Chalisa
intro: Shri Guru Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Guru
description:
|| Guru Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Om namo Gurudevji, sabke sarjan haar.
Vyapak antar bahar mein, Paar Brahma kartaar.
Devan ke bhi dev ho, simrun main baar-baar.
Aapki kirpa bina, hove na bhav se paar.
Rishi-muni sab sant jan, japen tumhara jaap.
Atmagyan ghat paay ke, nirbhaya ho gaye aap.

|| Chaupai ||
Om namo Gurudev dayala, bhaktjanon ke ho pratipala.
Par upkar dharo avtara, doobat jag mein hans ubara.
Tera darash karen badbhagi, jinki lagan Hari se lagi.
Naam jahaj tera sukhdayi, dhare jeev paar ho jaayi.
Paarbrahma Guru hain avinashi, shuddh swaroop sada sukhraashi.
Guru saman daata koi naahin, raja praja sab aas lagayi.
Guru sanmukh jab jeev ho jaave, koti kalp ke paap nasave.
Jin par kirpa Guru ki hoi, unko kami rahe nahin koi.
Hirday mein Gurudev ko dhare, Guru uska hain janm sanwaren.
Ram-Lakhan Guru seva jaani, vishwa-vijayi hue mahagyani.
Krishna Guru ki agya dhari, swayam jo Paarbrahma avtari.
Sadguru kirpa ati hai bhari, Narad ki chaurasi taari.
Kathin tapasya karen Shukdev, Guru bina nahin paya bhed.
Guru mile jab Janak Videhi, atmagyan maha sukh lehi.
Vyas, Vashishtha marm Guru jaani, sakal shastra ke bhaye ati gyani.
Anant rishi muni avtara, Sadguru charan-kamal chit dhara.
Sadguru naam jo hirday dhare, koti kalp ke paap nivare.
Sadguru seva ur mein dhare, ikkis peedhi apni vo taare.
Poorvjanm ki tapasya jaage, Guru seva mein tab man laage.
Sadguru-seva sab sukh hove, janam akarath kyon hai khove.
Sadguru seva birla jaane, moorakh baat nahin pahichane.
Sadguru naam japo din-raati, janm-janm ka hai yeh saathi.
Ann-dhan Lakshmi jo sukh chahe, Guru seva mein dhyan lagave.
Gurukirpa sab vighn vinashi, mite bharam aatam parkashi.
Poorv punya uday sab hove, man apna Sadguru mein khove.
Guru seva mein vighn padave, unka kul narkon mein jaave.
Guru seva se vimukh jo rehta, Yam ki maar sada vah sehta.
Guru vimukh bhoge dukh bhari, parmarth ka nahin adhikari.
Guru vimukh ko narak na thaur, baaten karo chahe lakh karod.
Guru ka drohi sabse boora, uska kaam hove nahin poora.
Jo Sadguru ka leve naam, vo hi paave achal aaram.
Sabhi sant naam se tariya, nigura naam bina hi mariya.
Yam ka doot door hi bhaage, jiska man Sadguru mein laage.
Bhoot, pishach nikat nahin aave, Gurumantra jo nishdin dhyave.
Jo Sadguru ki seva karte, daakan-shaakan sab hain darte.
Jantar-mantar, jaadu-tona, Guru bhakt ke kuch nahin hona.
Guru bhakt ki mahima bhari, kya samjhe nigura nar-nari.
Guru bhakt par Sadguru boothe, Dharamraj ka lekha chhoote.
Guru bhakt nij roop hi chahe, Guru marg se lakshya ko paave.
Guru bhakt sabke sir taj, unka sab devon par raj.

|| Doha ||
Yeh Sadguru Chalisa, padhe sune chitt laay.
Antar gyan prakash ho, daridrata dukh jaay.
Guru mahima beant hai, Guru hain param dayal.
Sadhak man anand kare, Guruvar karen nihal.

Significance of Guru Chalisa
Reciting the Guru Chalisa increases happiness and prosperity.
With the grace of Guru Chalisa, one attains wisdom, intelligence, wealth, strength, and spiritual knowledge.
Through the influence of Guru Chalisa, a person prospers and becomes wealthy.
They become a partner in all kinds of happiness and do not suffer.
By the mere grace of the Guru, a person is freed from all troubles and becomes radiant.

Guru Mantra
Gururbrahma Gururvishnuh Gururdevo Maheshwarah.
Guruh saakshaat param Brahma tasmai Shri Gurave namah.
Om Vedahi Guru Devay vidmahe param Guruve dhimahi tannoh Guruh prachodayat.
Om twama vaha vahai vada vai Gurauarchan gharai saha priyanharshetu.
Om Gurubhyo namah.
Om Dhivaray namah.
Om Gunine namah.

Guru Chalisa jo padhe, ur Guru dhyan lagay.
Janm-maran bhav dukh mite, kaal kabahun nahin khay.
Guru Chalisa padhe-sune, riddhi-siddhi sukh paay.
Man vanchhit kaaraj saren, janm safal ho jaay.
meaning:
|| Guru Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Om namo Gurudevji, sabke sarjan haar.
Om salutations to Gurudev, the Creator of all.
Vyapak antar bahar mein, Paar Brahma kartaar.
You are omnipresent, inside and out, the Supreme Creator.
Devan ke bhi dev ho, simrun main baar-baar.
You are the God of gods, I remember you again and again.
Aapki kirpa bina, hove na bhav se paar.
Without your grace, one cannot cross the ocean of existence.
Rishi-muni sab sant jan, japen tumhara jaap.
All sages, ascetics, and saints chant your name.
Atmagyan ghat paay ke, nirbhaya ho gaye aap.
By attaining self-knowledge in their hearts, they have become fearless.

|| Chaupai ||
Om namo Gurudev dayala, bhaktjanon ke ho pratipala.
Om salutations to the merciful Gurudev, the protector of devotees.
Par upkar dharo avtara, doobat jag mein hans ubara.
You took an incarnation for the benefit of others and saved the drowning world.
Tera darash karen badbhagi, jinki lagan Hari se lagi.
Only the fortunate, whose heart is connected to the Divine, get your vision.
Naam jahaj tera sukhdai, dhare jeev paar ho jaayi.
Your name is the ship of happiness, which helps the soul cross the ocean of life.
Paarbrahma Guru hain avinashi, shuddh swaroop sada sukhraashi.
The Guru is the immortal Supreme Brahma, the pure form, and the storehouse of happiness.
Guru saman daata koi naahin, raja praja sab aas lagayi.
There is no giver like the Guru; kings and subjects all place their hope in you.
Guru sanmukh jab jeev ho jaave, koti kalp ke paap nasave.
When a soul stands before the Guru, sins of millions of eons are destroyed.
Jin par kirpa Guru ki hoi, unko kami rahe nahin koi.
Those upon whom the Guru showers grace, lack nothing in life.
Hirday mein Gurudev ko dhare, Guru uska hain janm sanwaren.
He who holds the Gurudev in his heart, the Guru beautifies his life.
Ram-Lakhan Guru seva jaani, vishwa-vijayi hue mahagyani.
Ram and Lakshman understood the value of Guru's service and became world-conquering sages.
Krishna Guru ki agya dhari, swayam jo Paarbrahma avtari.
Krishna followed the Guru's command, even though He was the incarnation of the Supreme Brahma himself.
Sadguru kirpa ati hai bhari, Narad ki chaurasi taari.
The grace of the True Guru is very heavy (powerful); it ended the cycle of 84 (reincarnations) for Narada.
Kathin tapasya karen Shukdev, Guru bina nahin paya bhed.
Shukdev performed difficult penance, but without a Guru, he did not find the secret.
Guru mile jab Janak Videhi, atmagyan maha sukh lehi.
When Janak Videhi met his Guru, he attained the great happiness of self-knowledge.
Vyas, Vashishtha marm Guru jaani, sakal shastra ke bhaye ati gyani.
Vyas and Vashishtha understood the essence of the Guru and became great knowers of all scriptures.
Anant rishi muni avtara, Sadguru charan-kamal chit dhara.
Infinite sages and incarnations have held the lotus feet of the True Guru in their hearts.
Sadguru naam jo hirday dhare, koti kalp ke paap nivare.
Whoever holds the True Guru's name in their heart, their sins of millions of eons are removed.
Sadguru seva ur mein dhare, ikkis peedhi apni vo taare.
He who keeps the service of the True Guru in his heart, saves twenty-one generations of his lineage.
Poorvjanm ki tapasya jaage, Guru seva mein tab man laage.
Only when the penance of previous lives awakens, does the mind engage in Guru's service.
Sadguru-seva sab sukh hove, janam akarath kyon hai khove.
True Guru's service brings all happiness; why lose this life in vain?
Sadguru seva birla jaane, moorakh baat nahin pahichane.
Only a rare few know the service of the True Guru; fools do not recognize this truth.
Sadguru naam japo din-raati, janm-janm ka hai yeh saathi.
Chant the name of the True Guru day and night; He is the companion of all births.
Ann-dhan Lakshmi jo sukh chahe, Guru seva mein dhyan lagave.
Whoever wants food, wealth, prosperity, and happiness, should focus on Guru's service.
Gurukirpa sab vighn vinashi, mite bharam aatam parkashi.
Guru's grace destroys all obstacles; illusion is erased and the soul becomes enlightened.
Poorv punya uday sab hove, man apna Sadguru mein khove.
Only when past good deeds arise, does the mind get lost in the True Guru.
Guru seva mein vighn padave, unka kul narkon mein jaave.
Those who create obstacles in Guru's service, their lineage goes to hell.
Guru seva se vimukh jo rehta, Yam ki maar sada vah sehta.
He who remains turned away from Guru's service, always suffers the punishment of Yam (Death).
Guru vimukh bhoge dukh bhari, parmarth ka nahin adhikari.
One turned away from the Guru suffers great sorrow and is not worthy of spiritual salvation.
Guru vimukh ko narak na thaur, baaten karo chahe lakh karod.
One turned away from the Guru finds no place even in hell, no matter how much he talks.
Guru ka drohi sabse boora, uska kaam hove nahin poora.
The betrayer of the Guru is the worst; his work is never completed.
Jo Sadguru ka leve naam, vo hi paave achal aaram.
Whoever takes the name of the True Guru, attains permanent peace.
Sabhi sant naam se tariya, nigura naam bina hi mariya.
All saints crossed (the ocean of life) through the Name; those without a Guru died without the Name.
Yam ka doot door hi bhaage, jiska man Sadguru mein laage.
The messenger of death flees from the one whose mind is attached to the True Guru.
Bhoot, pishach nikat nahin aave, Gurumantra jo nishdin dhyave.
Ghosts and spirits do not come near one who meditates on the Guru's mantra day and night.
Jo Sadguru ki seva karte, daakan-shaakan sab hain darte.
Those who serve the True Guru, all witches and evil spirits fear them.
Jantar-mantar, jaadu-tona, Guru bhakt ke kuch nahin hona.
Black magic or sorcery has no effect on a devotee of the Guru.
Guru bhakt ki mahima bhari, kya samjhe nigura nar-nari.
The glory of a Guru's devotee is great; what will those without a Guru understand?
Guru bhakt par Sadguru boothe, Dharamraj ka lekha chhoote.
When the True Guru is pleased with a devotee, the account of the God of Justice (Dharamraj) is absolved.
Guru bhakt nij roop hi chahe, Guru marg se lakshya ko paave.
The devotee of the Guru seeks his own true self and attains the goal through the Guru's path.
Guru bhakt sabke sir taj, unka sab devon par raj.
The devotee of the Guru is the crown jewel of all; they have reign over all deities.

|| Doha ||
Yeh Sadguru Chalisa, padhe sune chitt laay.
Whoever reads or hears this Sadguru Chalisa with a focused mind.
Antar gyan prakash ho, daridrata dukh jaay.
Within them shines the light of knowledge, and poverty and sorrow vanish.
Guru mahima beant hai, Guru hain param dayal.
The glory of the Guru is infinite; the Guru is most merciful.
Sadhak man anand kare, Guruvar karen nihal.
The seeker's mind rejoices, and the Guru makes them fulfilled.

faq.q1: Why should the Guru Chalisa be recited?
faq.a1: A Guru is considered to be even higher than God. Reciting the Guru Chalisa is a way to express gratitude towards one's Guru, attain self-knowledge, and seek the right guidance in life.
faq.q2: What is the most auspicious day to read it?
faq.a2: Thursday (Guruvar) is considered the best day for Guru worship and the recitation of the Guru Chalisa. Reciting it on 'Guru Purnima' is especially fruitful.
faq.q3: Can I read it even if I don't have a specific Guru?
faq.a3: Yes, you can recite the Guru Chalisa while meditating on the 'Adi Guru' (Lord Shiva or the wisdom within yourself). It helps in spreading the light of knowledge and intellect.
faq.a4: Yes, reciting the Guru Chalisa reduces mental wandering and makes a person patient, humble, and focused.
faq.q5: Are there any specific rules for reading it?
faq.a5: In Guru devotion, 'feeling' (Bhav) is more important than rules. You can recite it at any time with full faith and surrender.
